title: Count Draynor
wikitext:
{{External|rs|rsc}} {{Otheruses|the quest boss fought during [[Vampyre Slayer]]|the [[Nightmare Zone]] variant|Count Draynor (Nightmare Zone)}} {{Infobox Monster |name = Count Draynor |image = [[File:Count Draynor.png|150px]] |release = [[28 January]] [[2001]] |update = New Quest: Vampire Slayer |members = No |combat = 34 |size = 1 |examine = Stop looking and run! |attributes = vampyre1 |xpbonus = -97.5 |max hit = 3 |aggressive = Yes |poisonous = No |attack style = [[Crush]] |attack speed = 4 |slayxp = 0.8 |cat = Vampyres |assignedby = mazchna,vannaka,chaeldar,nieve,duradel |hitpoints = 35 |att = 30 |str = 25 |def = 30 |mage = 1 |range = 1 |attbns = 0 |strbns = 0 |amagic = 0 |mbns = 0 |arange = 0 |rngbns = 0 |dstab = 2 |dslash = 1 |dcrush = 3 |dmagic = 0 |drange = 0 |immunepoison = Not immune |immunevenom = Not immune |immunecannon = No |immunethrall = No |id = 3481,3482 }} '''Count Draynor Drakan'''{{CiteNPC|npc=Malak|loc=Desert Treasure I|quote=[I am here only to serve the mighty Drakan. Yup, Drakan, he's the man.] I see. I would perhaps be more inclined to believe you if I could not smell the death blood of his brother Draynor upon you.}} lives in a [[coffin (Vampyre Slayer)|coffin]] in the basement of [[Draynor Manor]]. He was part of [[Vampyre Houses#House Drakan|House Drakan]], being the younger brother of [[Lowerniel Drakan|Lowerniel]], [[Ranis Drakan|Ranis]] and [[Vanescula Drakan]]. He can be fought in the [[Nightmare Zone]] after the quest [[Vampyre Slayer]] is complete. A stake is not necessary to kill him in the Nightmare Zone, as he will automatically die upon reaching 0 health. [[File:Fighting Count Draynor.png|thumb|left|A player fights against Count Draynor.]] ==History== Originally raised alongside his siblings on the vampyre homeworld of [[Vampyrium]], much of Draynor's history remains unknown. According to Vanescula, he liked to give himself various titles, but was considered to be cowardly by his siblings. He was eventually exiled from [[Morytania]] by Lowerniel and ended up west of the River Salve with his servant [[Ruantun]], which caused them to be significantly weakened. He is responsible for many deaths of avid adventurers and innocents who have fallen prey to his sharp fangs. Count Draynor is the target in the [[Vampyre Slayer]] quest which requires the player to kill him to save the people of [[Draynor Village]], who have been terrorised by Count Draynor for centuries. ==Strategy== The health recovery rate of Count Draynor is very high – about twenty times the rate of ordinary monsters. To balance his health regeneration, fighting him provides greatly reduced Combat experience. [[Garlic]] does not affect this health regeneration; rather, when the Count is at full health it deals 10 damage, reduces his attack and strength by 10 levels, and reduces his defence by 40 levels.{{CiteTwitter|url=https://twitter.com/JagexAsh/status/1221516088086867974|author=Mod Ash|date=26 January 2020|archiveurl=https://reldo.runescape.wiki/i/106526|archivedate=3 August 2020}} The garlic effect happens on auto-attack delay, so it will happen when using any melee combat but if using magic you must autocast spells with a staff to trigger it - manually casting spells will not work. Garlic will also increase all damage you deal by 1.{{CiteTwitter|author=Mod Ash|url=https://twitter.com/JagexAsh/status/1328824389815635973|date=17 November 2020|archiveurl=https://reldo.runescape.wiki/i/100959|archivedate=20 September 2021|quote=[Does bringing garlic to the Vampyre Slayer quest fight increase your max hit? ... I seem to be hitting +1 higher with garlic.] Yes. That's in addition to the one-off stat-reduction effect it has when the fight begins.}} Importantly, the debuff effect from garlic only lasts for about 30 seconds. Take advantage of the effective accuracy boost with hard hitting melee weapons while it lasts. Count Draynor can be harmed with ordinary [[weapon]]s, but cannot be killed by them. Once his health is low enough, players must drive a [[stake]] through his heart using a [[hammer]] in order to deal the final blow. If a player does not use the hammer and stake, he will heal to full health when his health points are low. Once the effect ends, low-level characters may struggle to surpass the high regeneration of the Count. Due to Count Draynor's low magic defence, [[Wind Strike]] is quite effective, especially upon reaching level 13 Magic for maximum damage. If players decide to kill him with magic, players should be advised to not wear metal armour until they come in to use the stake for the kill as they have substantial negative magic accuracy. With full magic gear, a level 9 mage can kill him by bringing about 10 pieces of tuna, depending on luck. ==Stat restoration== After being drained by the garlic, Count Draynor will restore both his hitpoints and drained levels at a rate of 1 point every 5 ticks.{{CiteDiscord|author=Mod Ash|url=https://discord.com/channels/324132423636090880/431834766325841920/1405120776255836261|date=13 August 2025|archiveurl=https://reldo.runescape.wiki/i/27962802#chat-messages-431834766325841920-1405120776255836261|archivedate=7 March 2026|quote=1. Yes.
2. 30 secs for most of them, restoring by +1 every 5 server ticks, hence 50 ticks = 30 secs. Restoring the -40 Defence takes longer.
N.B. The debuff is actually being checked every time you try to attack him, even if your action delay means that an attack won't happen, i.e. every server tick.
When the debuff is checked:
- It will drain his Attack by -10 _if_ it's full, and will tell you "The vampyre seems to weaken."
- It will drain his Strength by -10 _if_ it's full, silently.
- It will drain his Defence by -40 _if_ it's full, silently.
- It will drain his HP by -10 _if_ it's full, silently.
So:
- You're seeing the message every ~30 secs because that's how long it took his Attack stat to restore.
- Defence takes 4x longer to restore, and it'll be drained again if it does restore before he dies, but there isn't an additional message to tell you about the progress.|image=Ash Count Draynor garlic mechanics.png|ctximage=Ash Count Draynor garlic mechanics (query).png|ctxquote=(...) 1) Can I check if his passive regeneration also restores the drained stats by the garlic? 2) If it does, then how fast does he regenerate these stats back up again before the garlic reapplies its debuff in the next 30-second cycle?}} If the player is in possession of a garlic, and has an attack queued up against him, a check will be made every tick whether or not his stats have been restored back to full again. Assuming no other form of stat reduction is applied, the Count will restore his Attack back to full after 50 ticks, or 30 seconds. The garlic effect will then kick in again, reducing his Attack by 10 levels and printing the message: {{mes|The vampyre seems to weaken.}} The same applies for his Strength stat, except that the aforementioned message is not displayed once his stats are drained anew. Although garlic is programmed to reduce Count Draynor's Defence by 40 levels, he only has a baseline Defence level of 30, which is consequently reduced to zero. It will take 150 ticks (90 seconds) for his Defence to restore back to full, and the garlic effect to be reapplied. No message is printed when this occurs. Should Count Draynor's health restore back to full over the course of the fight, the garlic will deal 10 damage to him again. ==Changes== {{Subject changes header}} {{Subject changes |date = 7 February 2006 |update = Cabin Fever |change = The NPC was graphically updated with the rework of Vampyre Slayer. }} {{Subject changes footer}} ===Gallery (historical)=== {|style="text-align: center" cellpadding="15" |[[File:Count Draynor (historical).png|center|300x150px]] |[[File:Count Draynor.png|center|300x150px]] |- |29 March 2004 –
7 February 2006 |7 February 2006 –
present |} ==Trivia== *Despite being a [[Vampyre]], he lacks many of their usual immunities and exhibits strange weaknesses not seen in others of his kind. This may be a result of his passage across the blessed [[River Salve]] — a boundary known to weaken or repel creatures of darkness.{{CiteNPC|npc=Vanescula Drakan|quote=Ah yes, the great 'Count' Draynor. He loved his stupid titles. He was weak even before he got trapped on the wrong side of the Salve. A coward as well. There's a reason Lowerniel had him banished.|loc=Sins of the Father}} *The Count Draynor character is loosely based on Count Dracula from the 1897 horror novel of the same name. *He is the only real Vampyre to appear in [[free-to-play]] worlds, as [[Count Check]] merely pretends to be one. ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Vampyre Slayer}} {{Vampyres}} [[Category:Bosses]] [[Category:Deceased characters]] [[Category:Quest monsters]]
